page: Microsoft Azure E96-24ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Edsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 24 vCPUs, derived from 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

A defining characteristic of this instance is its substantial memory capacity of 768.0 GB, yielding a high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This memory configuration is paired with 5669 GB of local storage, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. There are no GPUs bundled with this server type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong memory subsystem performance, with uncached read-only large block memory bandwidth reaching 190.74 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 120.18 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Single-core performance is also notable, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2156.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 31.18 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core computational benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core (23970.13) and Geekbench multi-core (15275.0), fall into the average tier, reflecting the lower vCPU count relative to the massive memory footprint. For LLM inference, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 286.32 tokens/sec on a small 135M model, though performance scales down to an average 140.39 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96-24ds_v6 offers a high ratio of memory and local storage relative to its compute allocation, making it a specialized configuration for memory-bound workloads rather than raw compute-heavy tasks. The hardware profile is appropriate for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis (which achieved 33.51 million SET operations per second in testing), data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that require substantial local scratch space.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96-24ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv6 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz with 24 vCPUs (12 physical cores) and a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with an extensive 768.0 GB of RAM (32.0 GB per vCPU) and 5669 GB of local storage, with no bundled GPUs. Benchmark results highlight strong memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 190.74 GB/sec and writes at 120.18 GB/sec. Single-core performance is also strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2156.0, while multi-core metrics remain in the average tier. This configuration provides high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads. It is appropriate for large databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24ds_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring a 24-vCPU Intel Xeon processor, 768 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24ds_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 24 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an exceptional RAM-to-vCPU ratio and extensive local storage for demanding data and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 24 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
768.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB per vCPU ratio
5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong uncached memory bandwidth and single-core performance
Appropriate for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
